title: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission’s Defining Moment
description: We stand at a defining moment for the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) and for the future of nuclear energy in the U.S. American’s electricity demand is rising sharply due to artificial intelligence, data centers, and industrial growth, and our nation increasingly sees nuclear energy as essential to our energy security.
content:
By Ho Nieh

title: Microsoft launches new US$99 per month AI-focused software bundle
description: Microsoft Corp is launching a new bundle of workplace software with the aim of getting more people to use its artificial intelligence tools for the office.
content:
Microsoft Corp is launching a new bundle of workplace software with the aim of getting more people to use its artificial intelligence tools for the office.
The E7 bundle will cost US$99 (RM388) per user per month – a 65% price increase from Microsoft... [1329 chars]

title: Supawriter Announces Guide Presenting AI Content Writing Platforms for SEO and Digital Publishing Workflows
description: New York, United States, March 12, 2026 /MarketersMEDIA/ -- Supawriter announced the publication of a guide presenting a curated list of AI content writing platforms used for SEO-focused content creation and digital marketing workflows. The publication documents the role of artificial intelligence in content generation and outlines several tools and practices associated with automated writing systems and search engine optimization.

title: AI founders must move fast to stay on top of tech stacks: Resolve AI CEO Spiros Xanthos
description: With artificial intelligence maturing rapidly and demand from customers increasing significantly, AI startups need to move fast and stay on top of every layer of the technology stack from hardware to user experience, said Spiros Xanthos, founder and chief executive of Resolve AI, an agentic AI platform for software troubleshooting.

title: Atlassian to cut roughly 10% jobs in pivot to AI
description: March 11 (Reuters) - Atlassian said ⁠on Wednesday it would lay off around 10% of its workforce, or 1,600 ⁠employees, to push into artificial intelligence and enterprise sales.
content:

title: Electronics sector braces for tariff thump
description: As US tariffs and baht fluctuation pose challenges for the electronics industry, Thailand's major export earner, executives see investment and product diversification as key to cushion the impact, while demand for artificial intelligence (AI) and data centres continues to drive revenue growth.

title: AI Coding Startup Cursor in Talks for About $50 Billion Valuation
description: Cursor, a leading artificial intelligence startup for coding, is in talks with investors for a funding round that would value the startup at about $50 billion, nearly double the valuation it secured last fall, according to people familiar with the efforts.

title: ‘Convincing’ AI scams drove UK fraud cases to record 444,000 last year
description: Criminals using artificial intelligence tools to take over mobile, bank and online shopping accounts, says Cifas
content:
Criminals are increasingly exploiting AI technology to take over people’s mobile, banking and online shopping accounts, the UK’s leading anti-fraud body has warned.
Last year, a record number of scams were reported to the national fraud database, fue... [2541 chars]

title: OpenAI and Google engineers back Anthropic lawsuit over Pentagon AI blacklist
description: Nearly 40 tech staff and Microsoft warn supply chain risk label could hurt innovation and silence debate on military use of frontier artificial intelligence
content: Nearly 40 employees from OpenAI and Google have submitted an amicus brief supporting Anthropic’s lawsuit against the US government. The lawsuit challenges the constitutionality of the government’s decision to designate the AI firm as a supply-chain r... [1549 chars]

title: AI weather models show promise for hurricane forecasts, but new study finds key physical limitations
description: Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming weather prediction, enabling forecasts that once required hours of supercomputing time to run in just minutes. But as AI tools play an expanding role in high-stakes hazard modeling, researchers at Rice University say an essential question remains: Do AI-generated storms behave realistically?
